title: A New Chip to Save Energy in Data Centers
summary: Engineers at the University of California San Diego have made a new chip that could help save energy in data centers. Data centers are big buildings full of computers that use a lot of electricity to run things like video games and websites. The chip helps change high voltage electricity into lower voltage that computers need to work safely. In tests, the chip did this job very well, with 96.2% efficiency, meaning it wastes very little energy. This is important because data centers use more power as people use more digital stuff. The chip uses a special part called a piezoelectric resonator that vibrates to move energy, which is different from old chips that use magnets. This new design could make data centers smaller and use less electricity, helping the environment.

How efficient was the chip in tests?

background_read: Data centers are places with many computers that help run the internet, games, and apps. They need a lot of electricity, which can waste energy and harm the environment. Engineers are trying to make chips that use electricity better to reduce waste. This new chip from UC San Diego uses vibrations instead of magnets to change voltage, making it more efficient and helping save power in data centers.
Article_Structure: WHO: Engineers at the University of California San Diego
WHAT: Made a new chip that changes high voltage to low voltage efficiently
WHERE: In data centers, which are big buildings with computers
WHY: To save energy and reduce waste as digital demands grow

perspectives.description: The chip uses new technology to improve energy use in electronics
